enrichment.short_description: Reservation, waitlist, and table management software integrated with the Toast restaurant POS platform.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Toast Tables adds reservations, waitlists, and floor plan management to Toast POS, starting at $50/month with no per-cover fees.

features.title: Live floor plan sync
features.description: An automatically updated floor plan shows each table's status, including whether guests have ordered and if those orders have been fulfilled.

features.title: Waitlist & reservation booking
features.description: Guests can join a waitlist or book a table directly from the restaurant's website, Google, or the Local by Toast app.

features.title: Automated guest notifications
features.description: Automated messaging updates guests on seating status and table changes to help reduce no-shows.

features.title: Guest notes synced to POS
features.description: Guest notes, special requests, and visit history from Toast Tables and Guestbook flow automatically into the POS and handheld devices.

features.title: Server roster tracking
features.description: A synced server roster shows time since last seating, total covers, and active covers per server.

pros: Flat monthly pricing with no per-cover fees
Guest data and visit history flow directly into Toast POS and handhelds
Automated guest messaging helps cut down on no-shows
Booking available through the restaurant's own site, Google, and the Local by Toast app
cons: Designed to run within the Toast POS ecosystem rather than as a fully standalone tool
Entry-level tier caps reservations at 25 per month
Full pricing and reservation-heavy functionality requires the higher $199/month Plus tier

faqs.question: How much does Toast Tables cost?
faqs.answer: Toast Tables is priced at $50/month for restaurants seating on a first-come, first-served basis (unlimited waitlist, 25 reservations/month), or $199/month for Toast Tables Plus, which includes unlimited waitlist and reservations.

faqs.question: Does Toast Tables charge per-cover fees?
faqs.answer: No. Toast Tables is sold as a flat monthly price with no fees per cover.

faqs.question: Where can guests book a table?
faqs.answer: Guests can book directly from the restaurant's website, through Google, or via the Local by Toast mobile app.

faqs.question: Does Toast Tables work with Toast POS?
faqs.answer: Yes, guest notes, special requests, and visit history sync automatically between Toast Tables, Guestbook, and the Toast POS and handhelds.

use_cases.title: Walk-in-first restaurants
use_cases.description: Restaurants that primarily seat guests on a first-come, first-served basis can use the $50/month tier for waitlist management.

use_cases.title: Reservation-based dining
use_cases.description: Restaurants taking a high volume of reservations can use Toast Tables Plus for unlimited bookings and waitlist entries.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Toast Tables?
paragraphs: Toast Tables is a reservation, waitlist, and table management add-on built into the Toast restaurant point-of-sale platform, made by Boston-based Toast, Inc. It connects guest bookings, host workflows, floor plans, and seating data into a single system that syncs with the POS.

The product is sold in two flat-rate tiers: a $50/month option for restaurants that seat primarily on a first-come, first-served basis with an unlimited waitlist and 25 reservations per month, and a $199/month Toast Tables Plus tier with unlimited waitlist entries and reservations, both with no per-cover charges.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Toast Tables is for
paragraphs: Toast Tables is built for restaurants already running on, or considering, the Toast POS platform that want reservation and waitlist tools tied directly to their point-of-sale and guest data, rather than a separate standalone reservation system.

enrichment.short_description: Hospitality platform for reservations, guest CRM, and marketing automation, now owned by DoorDash.
enrichment.headquarters: New York City, New York, United States
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SevenRooms is a reservation, guest CRM, and marketing platform for restaurants, hotels, and venues, acquired by DoorDash in 2025.

features.title: Multi-channel reservations
features.description: Take reservations from the restaurant's own website, DoorDash Reservations, Deliveroo Reservations, Google, Instagram, and Yelp.

features.title: AI-powered table management
features.description: Smart seating, pacing controls, cover forecasting, and waitlist automation aimed at seating guests faster.

features.title: Voice AI for phone bookings
features.description: Voice technology designed to capture call-in reservations so calls aren't missed.

features.title: Guest data and CRM
features.description: Automatically captures and unifies guest data across every booking, visit, and order.

features.title: Marketing automation
features.description: Automated email, SMS, and campaign journeys designed to drive repeat visits.

features.title: Events and experiences management
features.description: Tools for managing ticketed events and reservation add-ons.

features.title: Reputation management
features.description: Aggregates guest feedback across channels.

pros: Distributes reservations through DoorDash and Deliveroo in addition to Google, Instagram, and Yelp
AI table management and voice AI aimed at reducing missed reservations
Serves a wide range of venue types beyond restaurants, including hotels, clubs, and wineries
Used by over 15,000 restaurants globally, per the company
cons: No public pricing; requires booking a demo to get a quote
Now part of DoorDash following its 2025 acquisition, which could shift product priorities
Enterprise-oriented feature set may be more than small independent restaurants need

faqs.question: Who owns SevenRooms?
faqs.answer: SevenRooms is owned by DoorDash, which completed its acquisition of the company in June 2025.

faqs.question: What types of venues use SevenRooms?
faqs.answer: SevenRooms serves restaurants, restaurant groups, hotels, membership clubs, nightclubs and bars, sports and entertainment venues, and breweries and wineries.

faqs.question: Does SevenRooms have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes. It includes AI-powered table management for seating optimization and Voice AI for handling call-in reservations.

faqs.question: Is SevenRooms pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, SevenRooms directs prospective customers to request a demo rather than publishing pricing on its site.

use_cases.title: Multi-location restaurant groups
use_cases.description: Unified guest data and marketing automation across multiple venues.

use_cases.title: Hotel food and beverage outlets
use_cases.description: Reservation and guest management for hotel restaurants and bars.

use_cases.title: Nightlife and entertainment venues
use_cases.description: Table and guest management for clubs, bars, and entertainment venues.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SevenRooms?
paragraphs: SevenRooms is a hospitality management platform combining reservations, guest data and CRM, marketing automation, and table operations tools. Founded in 2011 and headquartered in New York City, it was acquired by DoorDash in June 2025.

The platform distributes reservations across a restaurant's own website as well as DoorDash Reservations, Deliveroo Reservations, Google, Instagram, and Yelp, and layers in AI-powered table management and voice AI for phone bookings.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SevenRooms is for
paragraphs: SevenRooms is aimed at restaurants, restaurant groups, hotels, membership clubs, nightlife venues, sports and entertainment venues, and breweries and wineries that want reservations, guest data, and marketing unified in one platform.

enrichment.short_description: Global restaurant reservation platform, owned by Booking Holdings, used by more than 65,000 restaurants.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OpenTable for Restaurants offers reservations, table management, and diner insights, with plans from $149/month plus per-cover fees.

features.title: Online reservations and booking widget
features.description: Lets diners book directly through a restaurant profile, booking widget, or the OpenTable network.

features.title: Table and floor plan management
features.description: Live table availability, floor plans, guest notes, and waitlist management for front-of-house staff.

features.title: Guest insights and CRM
features.description: Detailed guest data to inform tailored hospitality, plus reports on cover trends, reservation sources, and guest spend.

features.title: Benchmarking
features.description: Compares a restaurant's performance against other local restaurants on metrics like seated guests and no-shows.

features.title: 200+ integrations
features.description: Connects with more than 200 restaurant tools.

features.title: Reminders and rewards management
features.description: Email reminders and rewards program management aimed at reducing no-shows and encouraging repeat visits.

pros: Access to OpenTable's large existing network of diners as part of Booking Holdings
Only charges per-cover fees for diners sourced through OpenTable's network on Core and Pro plans, not for direct bookings
Integrates with more than 200 restaurant tools
24/7 support from the company, with a reported 92% customer satisfaction score from restaurant partners
cons: Plans start at $149/month, with per-cover fees on top for diners discovered via OpenTable
Benchmarking and advanced reporting features require the higher Core or Pro tiers
As part of Booking Holdings, a large public company, the platform may offer less flexibility than smaller independent tools

faqs.question: How much does OpenTable cost?
faqs.answer: OpenTable for Restaurants pricing starts at $149/month for the Basic plan, with Core at $299/month and Pro at $499/month.

faqs.question: Does OpenTable charge per-cover fees?
faqs.answer: On the Core and Pro plans, OpenTable only charges cover fees for diners who discover the restaurant through OpenTable's website, app, or affiliate network; diners who book directly through the restaurant's own website or by phone aren't charged a fee.

faqs.question: Who owns OpenTable?
faqs.answer: OpenTable is owned by Booking Holdings, which acquired it in 2014.

faqs.question: How many restaurants use OpenTable?
faqs.answer: OpenTable states it helps more than 65,000 restaurants worldwide fill reservations.

use_cases.title: Independent restaurants seeking diner discovery
use_cases.description: Restaurants that want exposure to OpenTable's existing diner network and app.

use_cases.title: Multi-location groups wanting benchmarking
use_cases.description: Groups that want to compare performance across locations and against local competitors.

features: Per-cover fees only on OpenTable-sourced diners

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OpenTable?
paragraphs: OpenTable is a restaurant reservation and table management platform founded in 1998 and headquartered in San Francisco. It has been owned by Booking Holdings, the parent company of Priceline, since 2014.

The platform centralizes online reservations, live table availability, floor plans, guest notes, and waitlists, and integrates with more than 200 restaurant tools. Pricing runs from a $149/month Basic plan up to a $499/month Pro plan, with additional per-cover fees on Core and Pro plans for diners sourced through OpenTable's network.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OpenTable is for
paragraphs: OpenTable suits restaurants that want visibility to a large, established network of diners in addition to managing their own bookings, floor plans, and guest data in one system.

enrichment.short_description: Restaurant reservation and operations platform owned by American Express, with flat monthly pricing and no cover fees.
enrichment.headquarters: New York City, New York, United States
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Resy is a reservation and table management platform owned by American Express, with plans from $249/month and no per-cover fees.

features.title: Table and reservation management
features.description: Real-time reservation tracking and customizable booking parameters for front-of-house staff.

features.title: Web booking and mobile app
features.description: Guests can book through the restaurant's own site or the Resy consumer app.

features.title: Waitlist management
features.description: Automated walk-in waitlist tools alongside standard reservations.

features.title: Guest CRM
features.description: Guest profiles and dining history to support personalized service.

features.title: No-show protection
features.description: Credit card holds and deposits available to reduce no-shows.

features.title: Tock-powered plans
features.description: Essential and Premium tiers run on Tock's platform, adding advanced customization and event ticketing.

pros: Flat monthly fee with no per-cover charges on any plan
Backed by American Express, giving restaurants access to the Amex Card Member diner network
Offers both Resy-powered and Tock-powered plan tiers depending on feature needs
Used by roughly 16,000 restaurants globally
cons: Starting price of $249/month is higher than some competitors
Tock-powered Essential and Premium plans carry additional prepayment processing fees of 2-3%
Feature set is split across two different underlying platforms (Resy-branded vs. Tock-powered), which adds some complexity when choosing a plan

faqs.question: Does Resy charge per-cover fees?
faqs.answer: No. Resy charges one flat monthly fee per plan and does not charge fees on a per-reservation basis.

faqs.question: Who owns Resy?
faqs.answer: Resy was acquired by American Express in 2019, in a deal reported at approximately $400 million.

faqs.question: What plans does Resy offer?
faqs.answer: Resy offers Platform ($249/month) and Platform 360 ($399/month) plans powered by Resy, plus Essential ($269/month) and Premium ($399/month) plans powered by Tock, which carry prepayment fees of 3% and 2% respectively.

faqs.question: When was Resy founded?
faqs.answer: Resy was founded in 2014 by Gary Vaynerchuk, Ben Leventhal, and Michael Montero, and is headquartered in New York City.

use_cases.title: Restaurants seeking Amex diner exposure
use_cases.description: Restaurants that want visibility to American Express Card Member dining benefits and the Resy consumer network.

use_cases.title: Venues needing ticketed events
use_cases.description: Hospitality businesses that want to sell ticketed dining experiences via the Tock-powered plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Resy?
paragraphs: Resy is a restaurant reservation and operations platform founded in 2014 by Gary Vaynerchuk, Ben Leventhal, and Michael Montero, and headquartered in New York City. American Express acquired Resy in 2019.

The platform charges a flat monthly fee with no per-cover charges. It offers two Resy-branded plans (Platform and Platform 360) as well as two Tock-powered plans (Essential and Premium) that add event ticketing and carry separate prepayment processing fees.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Resy is for
paragraphs: Resy is built for restaurants, hospitality groups, hotels, and wineries that want reservation and guest management tools alongside exposure to American Express's dining network.

enrichment.short_description: Reservation and event ticketing platform for restaurants and wineries, now owned by American Express.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Tock is a flat-rate reservation and event ticketing platform with no cover fees, used in 30+ countries and owned by American Express.

features.title: No-Show Elimination Suite
features.description: Offers free, deposit, prepaid, and credit card hold reservation types to reduce no-shows.

features.title: Walk-in waitlist and table management
features.description: Included in Tock's base plan alongside guest and event management.

features.title: Event and experience ticketing
features.description: Lets venues sell reservations alongside unique experiences such as a chef's counter, cocktail class, or happy hour.

features.title: Guest notes and dining history
features.description: Guest notes, tags, and dining history to help personalize service.

features.title: Two-way SMS communication
features.description: Built-in SMS messaging with guests alongside chat and email support.

features.title: Third-party integrations
features.description: Integrates with Toast, WineDirect, Commerce7, Tripleseat, and Upserve, among others.

pros: Flat-rate pricing model with no per-cover fees
Deposit and prepayment tools built specifically to reduce no-shows
Supports ticketed dining experiences, not just standard reservations
Used by hospitality businesses in more than 30 countries
cons: Pricing is not published on the official site and requires contacting sales
Ownership has changed twice in recent years (sold to Squarespace in 2021, then acquired by American Express in 2024 from Squarespace)
Ticketed-experience features are geared more toward chef-driven and fine-dining concepts than casual restaurants

faqs.question: Who owns Tock?
faqs.answer: Tock was founded in 2014 by Nick Kokonas and Brian Fitzpatrick, sold to Squarespace in 2021, and acquired by American Express from Squarespace in 2024.

faqs.question: Does Tock charge per-cover fees?
faqs.answer: No, Tock uses a flat-rate pricing model and does not charge cover fees.

faqs.question: What is the No-Show Elimination Suite?
faqs.answer: It's Tock's set of reservation types, including free, deposit, prepaid, and credit card hold bookings, designed to reduce no-shows.

faqs.question: What does Tock integrate with?
faqs.answer: Tock integrates with platforms including Toast, WineDirect, Commerce7, Tripleseat, and Upserve.

use_cases.title: Fine dining with ticketed reservations
use_cases.description: Restaurants that sell reservations as tickets, including deposits or prepayment, to reduce no-shows.

use_cases.title: Wineries and experience-based venues
use_cases.description: Venues selling tastings, classes, or other ticketed experiences alongside standard bookings.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Tock?
paragraphs: Tock is a reservation, waitlist, and event ticketing platform founded in 2014 by Nick Kokonas and Brian Fitzpatrick in Chicago. It was sold to Squarespace in 2021 and acquired by American Express in 2024.

The platform is built around a No-Show Elimination Suite that lets restaurants require free, deposit, prepaid, or credit-card-hold reservations, and it supports selling ticketed experiences like chef's counters or classes alongside standard bookings. It integrates with tools including Toast, WineDirect, Commerce7, Tripleseat, and Upserve.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Tock is for
paragraphs: Tock is aimed at restaurants, wineries, and hospitality businesses that want to reduce no-shows through deposits and prepayment, or that sell ticketed dining experiences in addition to standard reservations.

enrichment.short_description: Restaurant and hotel reservation platform offering commission-free bookings, CRM, and marketing tools.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Eat App offers commission-free restaurant reservations, table management, and CRM, with plans starting at $99/month.

features.title: Commission-free online reservations
features.description: Accepts bookings from Instagram, Google, and the restaurant's website without commission charges.

features.title: Table management
features.description: Visual table management tools to help fill more seats and turn tables faster.

features.title: Guest database and CRM
features.description: Builds a guest database automatically from reservation and visit activity.

features.title: Automated confirmations and reminders
features.description: Sends reservation confirmations and reminders to save staff time.

features.title: Pay-at-table via QR code
features.description: Payment processing at the table through QR codes.

features.title: Email marketing suite
features.description: Built-in email marketing with campaign capabilities.

features.title: WhatsApp messaging
features.description: Integrated WhatsApp messaging with guests.

features.title: Review management
features.description: Post-dining surveys and review collection tools.

pros: Reservations from Instagram, Google, and the restaurant's own site are commission-free
Available on iPhone, Android, web browsers, and iPad
Plans scale from a 300-cover Starter tier to unlimited covers on Essential and Pro
Pro tier includes a dedicated success manager
cons: Starter plan is capped at 300 covers per month
Some marketing and loyalty tools are limited to higher-priced tiers
AI is listed as a feature on the site but specific AI capabilities aren't detailed

faqs.question: How much does Eat App cost?
faqs.answer: Eat App's plans are Starter at $99/month (up to 300 covers/month), Essential at $199/month (unlimited covers), and Pro at $389/month (unlimited covers plus a dedicated success manager).

faqs.question: Does Eat App charge commission on reservations?
faqs.answer: No, Eat App advertises commission-free reservations from Instagram, Google, and the restaurant's website.

faqs.question: What platforms does Eat App support?
faqs.answer: Eat App is available synced across iPhone, Android, web browsers, and iPad.

faqs.question: Where is Eat App based?
faqs.answer: Eat App is headquartered in Dubai, United Arab Emirates, and was founded in 2015.

use_cases.title: Independent restaurants and cafes
use_cases.description: Restaurants, cafes, and bars managing reservations and walk-ins without paying commission.

use_cases.title: Hotel food and beverage outlets
use_cases.description: Hotels and hospitality groups managing restaurant reservations across properties.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Eat App?
paragraphs: Eat App is a restaurant and hotel reservation management platform founded in 2015 and headquartered in Dubai. It covers reservation management, marketing, reviews, and a payment suite in one system.

The platform accepts commission-free bookings from Instagram, Google, and a restaurant's own website, and is available synced across iPhone, Android, web browsers, and iPad. Pricing runs from a $99/month Starter plan capped at 300 covers to a $389/month Pro plan with unlimited covers and a dedicated success manager.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Eat App is for
paragraphs: Eat App is built for restaurants, cafes, bars, breweries, nightclubs, and hotel food and beverage operations that want commission-free reservations alongside CRM and marketing tools.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based table booking system for independent restaurants, with all features included on every plan.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Tablein is a cloud table booking system for restaurants, with plans from $79/month and no commission or add-on fees.

features.title: Digital reservation book
features.description: Calendar views and floor plan visualization of the restaurant's table layout.

features.title: Online booking widget
features.description: An embeddable reservation tool for the restaurant's website, allowing bookings even when the restaurant is closed.

features.title: Guest database
features.description: Tracks guest preferences, dietary restrictions, past orders, and visit frequency.

features.title: Automated notifications
features.description: Booking confirmations, reminders, and automated email/chat replies to reduce no-shows.

features.title: Payment integration
features.description: Integrates with Stripe and other payment gateways for deposits and no-show fees.

features.title: Website builder
features.description: Builds a restaurant website including food and drink menus.

features.title: Reporting and analytics
features.description: Dashboards and data reports to support business decisions.

pros: All plans include the full feature set with no add-on fees or commission charges
Used by restaurants, bars, and clubs in more than 50 countries
Named 'Best Booking System' for 2023 by Investopedia
Used by hotel brands including Hilton and Corinthia Hotels
cons: Starter and Growth plans charge per-reservation overage fees once the included booking count is exceeded
No AI features are mentioned on the site
Smaller brand recognition than larger competitors such as OpenTable or Resy

faqs.question: How much does Tablein cost?
faqs.answer: Tablein has three plans: Starter at roughly €67/month ($79 USD) with 50 reservations included, Growth at roughly €117/month ($139 USD) with 150 reservations included, and Success at roughly €177/month ($209 USD) with unlimited reservations.

faqs.question: Does Tablein charge commission on bookings?
faqs.answer: No, Tablein states its plans include all features with no additional paid add-ons or commission charges.

faqs.question: What payment processors does Tablein support?
faqs.answer: Tablein integrates with Stripe and other payment gateways for deposits and no-show fees.

faqs.question: Where is Tablein based?
faqs.answer: Tablein was founded in 2015 and is headquartered in Kaunas, Lithuania.

use_cases.title: Independent restaurants replacing paper booking
use_cases.description: Restaurants moving from a paper reservation book to a digital, multi-device system.

use_cases.title: Hotel restaurants and membership clubs
use_cases.description: Hospitality venues needing table booking alongside a guest database.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Tablein?
paragraphs: Tablein is a cloud-based table booking system founded in 2015 and headquartered in Kaunas, Lithuania. It digitizes reservation management for restaurants, replacing paper booking systems with tools accessible by staff across devices.
Every plan, from the €67/month Starter tier to the €177/month unlimited Success tier, includes the same full feature set with no separate add-on fees or commission charges.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Tablein is for
paragraphs: Tablein targets individual restaurants, restaurant groups, hotel restaurants, bars, pubs, clubs, and event or catering businesses looking for an affordable, full-featured booking system.

name: Quandoo
skip_reason: Quandoo announced in March 2026 that it is shutting down its restaurant booking platform in a planned, orderly wind-down: new bookings stop September 30, 2026, and the site/app/infrastructure fully shuts down December 31, 2026, after parent Recruit Holdings cited cumulative losses exceeding 260 million euros. The website itself shows the entity as 'Quandoo GmbH i.L.' (in liquidation/wind-down). Since the product is discontinued rather than an active, ongoing tool, it does not meet the bar for standard enrichment.

enrichment.short_description: Restaurant management and reservation software from TheFork, owned by TripAdvisor and used across Europe.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: TheFork Manager is TripAdvisor-owned restaurant management software with reservations, no-show tools, and analytics, priced by quote.

features.title: Smart floorplan
features.description: Table optimization tools built into a digital floorplan.

features.title: No-show reduction tools
features.description: Credit card guarantees and pre-payments to reduce no-shows.

features.title: Customizable booking flow
features.description: Configurable reservation flow for the restaurant's booking process.

features.title: Performance analytics with peer comparisons
features.description: Advanced analytics including comparisons against peer restaurants.

features.title: AI-powered review summaries
features.description: Guest communication tools including AI-generated summaries of reviews.

features.title: Multi-channel booking integration
features.description: Bookings integrated across Google, Tripadvisor, MICHELIN, and Meta, among others.

features.title: Guest data collection
features.description: Builds a guest database to support loyalty and retention.

pros: Direct bookings from the restaurant's own website, Instagram, and Facebook are included at no extra fee
Distribution through TheFork's consumer platform, reported at 20M+ monthly visits, and the TripAdvisor network
Used by 2,500+ Michelin-listed restaurants and 55,000 restaurant partners overall, per the company
Enterprise tier adds multi-location management and centralized analytics
cons: Pricing isn't published for either plan; both require contacting TheFork Manager for a quote
Bookings sourced from TheFork, TripAdvisor, and partner platforms are charged as a percentage of the average bill per guest
Primary market focus is Europe, operating across 11 European countries

faqs.question: How is TheFork Manager priced?
faqs.answer: TheFork Manager offers Performance and Enterprise plans, both priced by custom quote. Bookings from TheFork, TripAdvisor, and partner platforms are charged as a percentage of the average bill per guest, while bookings from the restaurant's own website, Instagram, and Facebook are included.

faqs.question: Who owns TheFork Manager?
faqs.answer: TheFork Manager is operated by LaFourchette SAS, which was acquired by TripAdvisor in 2014.

faqs.question: What channels does TheFork Manager integrate with?
faqs.answer: It integrates with Google, Tripadvisor, MICHELIN, and Meta, among other channels.

faqs.question: Does TheFork Manager support multiple locations?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Enterprise plan adds multi-location management, centralized analytics, and cross-venue guest data sharing.

use_cases.title: Restaurants wanting TheFork/TripAdvisor exposure
use_cases.description: Independent restaurants looking to be discovered through TheFork's diner platform and TripAdvisor.

use_cases.title: Multi-location restaurant groups
use_cases.description: Groups needing centralized data and analytics across venues.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is TheFork Manager?
paragraphs: TheFork Manager is restaurant management software from TheFork, operated by Paris-based LaFourchette SAS, which was acquired by TripAdvisor in 2014. It combines table reservations, order management, and service coordination.

The product is sold in two custom-quoted tiers, Performance and Enterprise, with fees on bookings sourced from TheFork, TripAdvisor, and partner platforms based on a percentage of the average bill, while direct bookings from the restaurant's own channels are included.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who TheFork Manager is for
paragraphs: TheFork Manager is aimed at restaurants operating in Europe, from new independents to Michelin-starred establishments and multi-location groups, that want to be discovered through TheFork's diner platform and TripAdvisor.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based restaurant POS from Lightspeed Commerce, deployed in about 150,000 locations worldwide.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Lightspeed Restaurant is a cloud POS for order management, inventory, and multi-location restaurants, priced by custom quote.

features.title: Online, tableside, and QR ordering
features.description: Multiple order channels including online ordering, tableside ordering, and QR code ordering.

features.title: Multi-location management
features.description: Manage multiple locations or revenue centers from one platform.

features.title: Offline functionality
features.description: Continues to operate if the restaurant experiences connectivity issues.

features.title: Kitchen Display System
features.description: Connects front-of-house and back-of-house teams.

features.title: Ingredient-level inventory tracking
features.description: Tracks inventory down to the ingredient level.

features.title: Real-time data analytics
features.description: Insights for menu optimization and customer behavior tracking.

features.title: Third-party integrations
features.description: Integrates with tools including 7shifts for scheduling and OpenTable for reservations.

pros: Continues working offline during connectivity issues
Used by more than 200 Michelin-starred restaurants, per the company
Deployed in roughly 150,000 locations across more than 100 countries
Runs on iPad-based POS hardware with a cloud backend
cons: Specific plan pricing is not published; Starter, Essential, and Premium tiers require contacting sales
Details on 'Lightspeed AI' capabilities are not disclosed on the product page
Some product marketing images are AI-generated or AI-edited rather than actual product screenshots, per the site's own disclosure

faqs.question: How much does Lightspeed Restaurant cost?
faqs.answer: Lightspeed does not publish specific prices for its Starter, Essential, or Premium restaurant POS plans on this page; pricing requires contacting sales.

faqs.question: Does Lightspeed Restaurant work offline?
faqs.answer: Yes, the system can continue to operate if the restaurant experiences internet connectivity issues.

faqs.question: What does Lightspeed Restaurant integrate with?
faqs.answer: It integrates with tools such as 7shifts for scheduling and payroll and OpenTable for reservations, among a wider partner network.

faqs.question: Who makes Lightspeed Restaurant?
faqs.answer: Lightspeed Restaurant is made by Lightspeed Commerce, founded in 2005 and headquartered in Montreal, Quebec, Canada.

use_cases.title: Multi-location restaurant groups
use_cases.description: Managing multiple locations or revenue centers from a single platform.

use_cases.title: Fine dining and Michelin-starred kitchens
use_cases.description: Restaurants needing detailed inventory and kitchen display coordination.

use_cases.title: Quick-service and cafe operations
use_cases.description: Venues needing QR code and tableside ordering options.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Lightspeed Restaurant?
paragraphs: Lightspeed Restaurant is a cloud-based point-of-sale system for restaurants made by Lightspeed Commerce, founded in 2005 and headquartered in Montreal, Quebec, Canada. It covers order management, payment processing, and inventory tracking.

The system supports online, tableside, and QR code ordering, multi-location management, an offline mode for connectivity issues, and ingredient-level inventory tracking, with pricing organized into Starter, Essential, and Premium tiers priced by custom quote.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Lightspeed Restaurant is for
paragraphs: Lightspeed Restaurant serves full-service and quick-service restaurants, fine dining, bars, breweries, cafes, and hotels, including more than 200 Michelin-starred restaurants and roughly 150,000 locations worldwide, per the company.
